task_completion.description: The office floor plan image is imported into the draw.io canvas.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The draw.io canvas displays the office floor plan image (showing rooms like Lobby, Conference Room, Server Room, and Bullpen).

task_completion.description: The diagram is organized into the three required layers.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The draw.io Layers panel is visible in at least one screenshot and shows exactly three layers named 'Floor Plan', 'Hardware', and 'Coverage'.

task_completion.description: Three Wireless Access Point icons are placed at the specified locations.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Three distinct icons representing Wireless Access Points are placed on the floor plan, specifically located in the Lobby, Conference Room, and Bullpen areas.

task_completion.description: Signal range circles are created for each access point.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Three circular shapes are drawn on the canvas, each surrounding one of the Access Point icons to represent WiFi coverage.
The coverage circles have reduced opacity (less than 100%), allowing the underlying floor plan details (walls, room labels) to be clearly seen through the circle's fill color.

task_completion.description: The working file is saved and the final heatmap is exported as a PNG to the correct directories.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show save/export dialogs, a file manager, or terminal commands confirming that 'wifi_project.drawio' is saved in '~/Diagrams/' and 'wifi_coverage_map.png' is saved in '~/Diagrams/exports/'.

integrity.description: The agent used the draw.io graphical interface to create the diagram.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show active interaction with the draw.io GUI (toolbars, canvas, layer panel, shape libraries) rather than generating the XML file via a text editor or script.

integrity.description: The agent used the provided floor plan image rather than drawing a fake one.
integrity.visual_evidence: The background image matches a standard generated office floor plan with specific rooms (Lobby, Conference Room, Server Room, Bullpen) rather than manually drawn basic rectangles.

integrity.description: The exported PNG was generated via draw.io's export function.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen using File -> Export as -> PNG in draw.io, or the final exported image perfectly matches the visual state of the draw.io canvas.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The floor plan image is an 800x600 PNG containing labeled rooms: Lobby, Conference Room, Server Room, and Bullpen/Open Office. The specs require placing exactly 3 Access Points in the Lobby, Conference Room, and Bullpen. The draw.io layers must be named exactly 'Floor Plan', 'Hardware', and 'Coverage'. The coverage circles must have an opacity of less than 100% (ideally 30-50%) so the floor plan is visible beneath them. The final files must be saved to ~/Diagrams/wifi_project.drawio and ~/Diagrams/exports/wifi_coverage_map.png.
